method_atom: A statistical-mechanics framework for critical phenomena and phase transitions on structured substrates with heterogeneous connectivity and small diameters, treating both transitions in the substrate's own architecture (emergence of a giant connected component, percolation and k-core percolation, condensation transitions) and symmetry-breaking or threshold transitions of many interacting units placed on that substrate (collective ordering, absorbing/epidemic thresholds, synchronization, self-organized criticality), including coupled feedback between substrate and units and strong finite-size effects.
object_atom: Complex networks — graphs with small diameters and complex, heterogeneous architectures — treated both as the object whose connectivity structure undergoes transitions and as substrates hosting interacting agents such as spins, epidemic spreaders, and coupled oscillators that mutually influence the network.
brief_reason: The critical-phenomena, percolation, and cooperative-transition machinery on structured substrates is the transferable method, while the substrate being complex networks (and the spins/epidemics/oscillators living on them) is the domain-specific object.
